content: Just as the high school English vocabulary framework frames 'reflexion' as a tripartite concept of reflecting external light, reflecting reality, and deliberate internal reflection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate all three analogous functions instead of only focusing on internal critique of outputs. This tripartite framing would allow LLMs to reflect external human user feedback as 'light', reflect mismatches between generated outputs and real-world constraints as 'reality', and then conduct deliberate self-correction to improve performance.
